title: Inbound PayStream payment webhook handler
trigger: PayStream posts a payment event to /webhooks/paystream
success_outcome: The payment event is validated, persisted, and the corresponding order is updated
tldr: Validate and persist PayStream payment webhook events, update order payment status

in: Signature validation of incoming webhook requests
Persist payment events and update order payment status

behavior: On POST /webhooks/paystream, validate the HMAC-SHA256 signature header, parse the event, upsert the payment record, and update the referenced order's payment status. Respond 200 on success; PayStream retries on non-2xx.
business_rules:
- INVALID_SIGNATURE: reject requests with a missing or invalid HMAC-SHA256 signature with 401.
- UNKNOWN_ORDER: reject events referencing an order id not present in the system with 422 UNKNOWN_ORDER.
- DUPLICATE_EVENT: idempotently accept replayed events; return 200 without reprocessing when the event id was already handled.
acceptance_scenarios: Given a valid signed payment event for a recognised order, when posted, then the order payment status is updated and 200 is returned
Given a request with an invalid signature, when posted, then 401 INVALID_SIGNATURE is returned
Given an event referencing an order not in the system, when posted, then 422 UNKNOWN_ORDER is returned
Given a previously processed event id, when posted again, then 200 is returned without duplicate processing
inputs_outputs_errors: Input: POST /webhooks/paystream with X-PayStream-Signature header and JSON body {eventId, orderId, amount, currency, status}. Output: 200 OK. Errors: 401 INVALID_SIGNATURE, 422 UNKNOWN_ORDER.
definition_of_done: Signature validation is tested with valid and tampered payloads
Idempotency is verified by replaying an event
security: All incoming requests must carry a valid HMAC-SHA256 signature computed over the raw request body using the shared PayStream webhook secret. Requests with missing or invalid signatures are rejected with 401 before any processing.
